RED: Wrote two failing tests in scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js FIRST (registered + run, confirmed RED before implementing):

  - testAdaptiveGeneratedPortSplit431 — a plan declaring a GENERATED_AGGREGATOR canonical (scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js) WITHOUT its codex twin + both forge ports in the SAME node must refuse with typed `generated_port_split`; the bundled shape (all 4 in one node) must pass; the gate must be inert without an edition-sync.js anchor. GREEN: Implemented in the canonical scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js, then synced byte-identical to the codex twin and rename-generated to both forge ports via `node scripts/edition-sync.js --write` (`--check` passes: 12 forge aggregator ports in rename-normalized parity; canon==codex byte-identical):

  - #431: anchor-gated require('./edition-sync') (GENERATED_AGGREGATORS + forgeRel); a freeze-wall after the FILE_CEILING check refuses `generated_port_split` when a node declares scripts/<base> (base in GENERATED_AGGREGATORS) without its codex twin + gitlab + gitea ports, naming the missing siblings. Gated on scripts/edition-sync.js existing under the validated root AND the module being require-able — silently inert in forge/codex/user installs.
